title: Advanced relative clauses
explanation:
Advanced relative clauses

At this level, you should distinguish defining and non-defining clauses, use prepositions with relatives, and avoid repetition.

  • The book, which I borrowed from Ana, was excellent.
  • The person to whom I spoke was very helpful.
  • This is the hotel where we stayed.

questions.text: My laptop, [*which|that|where] I bought last year, is already broken.
questions.feedback: Non-defining clauses use which, not that.

questions.text: The colleague [*who|which|whose] sits opposite me speaks four languages.
That is the company [*for which|for that|for where] she works.
questions.feedback: Preposition + which in a formal structure.

questions.text: The writer [*whose|which|where] latest novel won a prize is giving a talk tonight.
This is the village [*where|which|whose] my grandparents were born.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: The people, ____ were all volunteers, worked through the night. | *who | that | where
The programme ____ I was listening has just finished. | *which | who | where
The teacher to ____ I sent the email replied immediately. | *whom | which | where
The office, ____ is in the city centre, is easy to reach. | *which | where | whose
The candidate [*who I interviewed|which I interviewed|where I interviewed] seemed confident.
questions.feedback: Who as object is acceptable here.

questions.text: The website, [*which was redesigned|that was redesigned|where was redesigned] last month, looks better now.
The woman [*whose car was stolen|which car was stolen|where car was stolen] called the police.
That was the moment [*when|which|whose] everything changed.
The neighbourhood [*in which|where that|which in] they live is very quiet.
